summary: iter20 — Frontend retest of iter19 blockers. The one-line EmergencyLockdownPill import added by main agent at /app/frontend/src/pages/Operator.jsx:32 has fully resolved the crash. End-to-end UI flow verified against the live preview env (https://tbc-self-copy.the old preview host): operator login → setup-2fa skip → /operator?tab=settings loads with NO runtime-error-boundary. All 5 AppSettingsCard selectors render, AppSettingsCard sits ABOVE 'New user defaults' (verified via compareDocumentPosition), banner toggle flips backend banner_enabled:true, preview panel renders with current banner text, lockdown toggle (with auto-accepted window.confirm) flips login_lockdown_enabled:true, the EmergencyLockdownPill in the header flips BOTH flags in a single click + accepted confirm, pill changes from 'Lock app' to 'App is private' with the animate-pulse + red-500 styling, and a second click returns BOTH flags to false (no confirm required for release). End-of-run cleanup PATCH succeeded — final state is {banner_enabled:false, login_lockdown_enabled:false}. Preview env is clean.
critical_code_review_comments: Operator.jsx now correctly imports EmergencyLockdownPill (line 32) — crash fixed. Strongly recommend the lint guardrail mentioned in iter19 (enable `no-undef` / `react/jsx-no-undef` at ESLint level) so a missing import for a JSX-rendered component fails CI rather than only blowing up at first render.

EmergencyLockdownPill.jsx is concise and well-scoped: single PATCH flips both flags, optimistic state update, sonner toast, confirm() on engage only (release is one-click as designed), and a 30s background poll that pauses when the document is hidden — good citizen.

AppSettingsCard.jsx toggleBanner and toggleLockdown both rollback local state on failure — solid UX. Save button is wired to the textarea-driven save() which is correct; the standalone Switch toggles run their own PATCHes.
SettingsTab orders AppSettingsCard FIRST (verified via compareDocumentPosition in the playwright run) — matches the iter19 design intent.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all 19 retest assertions PASS (no error boundary, pill renders, all 5 AppSettingsCard selectors render, card-is-first ordering, banner toggle backend persistence, preview panel renders, lockdown toggle + window.confirm flow, pill flips both flags with red-pulse style, pill release returns both to false, end-of-run cleanup confirmed)

test_credentials: Operator: rac.investments.swe@gmail.com / REDACTED — login OK, 2FA setup page shows 'Skip' button which is clicked to land on /operator. Test ran from preview env via REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.
seed_data_creation: None — test used existing operator account and only flipped global app_settings flags. Final cleanup PATCH leaves the doc at {banner_enabled:false, login_lockdown_enabled:false}. banner_text retains the value previously written by iter19 tests ('ITER19 TEST: personal use only!') — this is harmless because banner_enabled is false; main agent may want to PATCH banner_text back to the production default if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter19 + iter20 are GREEN end-to-end. Operator console no longer crashes, AppSettingsCard + EmergencyLockdownPill are fully integrated. If you re-test, remember: (1) login redirects to /setup-2fa — click the 'Skip' button before navigating; (2) on /operator there's a first-time Quick Tour — call `get_by_role('button', name='Skip tour').click(force=True)`; (3) the pill engage flow uses window.confirm — register a `page.once('dialog', lambda d: asyncio.create_task(d.accept()))` BEFORE clicking; release is a single click with no confirm; (4) ALWAYS finish with a PATCH to {banner_enabled:false, login_lockdown_enabled:false} so the preview env stays open.
rca_of_the_issue: iter19 RCA stands: ReferenceError because <EmergencyLockdownPill /> at Operator.jsx:152 was never imported. Main agent added `import EmergencyLockdownPill from '../components/EmergencyLockdownPill';` at Operator.jsx:32 (between PreviewWidget and AnalyticsTab imports). Verified by reading the file AND by the full end-to-end UI flow running without hitting the runtime-error-boundary.
